title: MCP-Unique Repo-Set Fixture
description: Schema for repo-set fixtures specifying which repositories are available locally vs via MCP only. Supports cross-org discovery (different GitHub orgs). Cross-host (different code hosts) is deferred.

repo_set_id.description: Unique identifier for this repo set (e.g. 'kubernetes-ecosystem'). Used as reference in use case registry.

description.description: Human-readable description of this repo set and its purpose.

repos.description: List of all repositories in this fixture.

items.description: Full name (org/repo_name) of a repo available via local checkout in baseline config.

local_checkout_repos.description: Repos available locally in baseline config. Subset of repos[].full_name values.

items.description: Full name (org/repo_name) of a repo only accessible via MCP/Sourcegraph.

mcp_only_repos.description: Repos only accessible via MCP tools. Baseline agents cannot access these.

cross_org.description: True when repos span multiple GitHub organizations. Enables cross-org discovery tasks.

language_mix.description: Programming languages present across the repos in this fixture.

primary_language.description: The dominant programming language across the repo set.

host.description: Code host domain. Defaults to github.com. Cross-host support deferred.

org.description: Organization or owner on the code host (e.g. 'kubernetes', 'grafana').

full_name.description: Combined org/repo_name (e.g. 'kubernetes/kubernetes'). Used as key in local_checkout_repos and mcp_only_repos.

revision.description: Git ref to pin this repo to (commit SHA, tag, or branch). Ensures reproducibility.

logical_name.description: Human-friendly name for this repo's role in the fixture (e.g. 'core-api-server', 'go-client-library').

access_mode.description: How the agent can access this repo. 'local_checkout' = available in workspace, 'mcp_only' = only via Sourcegraph MCP, 'both' = available both ways.

sourcegraph_indexed.description: Whether this repo is indexed in Sourcegraph (either natively or via mirror).

sourcegraph_mirror.description: Name of the sg-benchmarks mirror if the public repo is not natively indexed (e.g. 'sg-benchmarks/kubernetes-client-go'). Only needed when sourcegraph_indexed=true via a mirror.
